translation.ABOUT_APP_TEXT:
This tool is designed to help you navigate and implement the best practice recommendations for body composition (BC) considerations.
 
There are other useful resources linked throughout the tool to help support you in your practice. Please note this resource is not designed to replace conversations with relevant individuals but rather support.
 
We encourage you to use this tool as part of your conversations and to support decision making about body composition (BC) in sport. You may reach a point where it is not recommended that you proceed with the body composition process. Please take note of the guidance and consider how you could meet the recommendations in order to proceed.
translation.ALT_REDICON: End of decision tree - Danger Icon
translation.GUIDELINE_CLICK: Click the numbers to read the guidelines
translation.G1_TEXT:
BC assessment should only be conducted with clear justification and informed consent, particularly if there's risk of harm.
 
It must not proceed without a valid medical or performance-related reason, especially where eating disorders or body image issues exist.
translation.G2_TEXT: Athletes must receive clear information and voluntarily agree to participate, with their consent properly documented.
translation.G3_TEXT:
Assessments must be carried out by trained professionals.
 
The chosen method should be based on reliability, accessibility, and athlete-specific needs.
translation.G4_TEXT:
Standardized protocols must be followed to ensure valid data.
 
Athletes should be informed, feel safe, and have the chance to ask questions. Data must be collected in secure, private conditions.
translation.G5_TEXT: Results should be interpreted carefully with input from the athlete support team to ensure safe and purposeful application.
translation.G6_TEXT: Results must be clearly presented with individual values and error margins. Normative values should not be the primary comparison for individual athletes.
translation.G7_TEXT: Athletes have the right to refuse data sharing. Where shared, results must be communicated respectfully, privately, and stored securely in accordance with privacy laws.
translation.G8_TEXT:
Follow-up assessments must align with athlete goals and readiness.
 
Ongoing BC assessments should only continue when clearly appropriate and agreed upon.
translation.TIP1: Swipe left for no and right for yes to answer the questions more efficiently!
translation.ICON_T1: Above, you can see which of the 8 steps you are at in the process.
translation.ICON_T2: Below, you can see the percentage of the process that is complete.
translation.ABOUT_APP_PAGE_TEXT: This tool was developed as a bachelor project to support navigation and implementation of recommended best practices for body composition (BC) assessment. The guidelines are based on the article below, and Therese Fostervold Mathisen is the project owner.
translation.ABOUT_APP_ARTICLE_TITLE: Mathisen, T. F., Ackland, T., Burke, L. M., Constantini, N., Haudum, J., Macnaughton, L. S., Meyer, N. L., Mountjoy, M., Slater, G., & Sundgot-Borgen, J. (n.d.). Best practice recommendations for body composition considerations in sport to reduce health and performance risks: A critical review, original survey and expert opinion by a subgroup of the IOC consensus on Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (REDs).
translation.HELP_PAGE_INTRO: This app guides you step by step through an assessment of whether it is appropriate to carry out a BC assessment.
translation.HELP_PAGE_ANSWERS: Answer the questions with Yes or No. Based on your answers, you will either continue, receive a recommendation to proceed with caution, or be advised not to continue.
translation.HELP_GREEN_TEXT: You can continue to the next step.
translation.HELP_YELLOW_TEXT: Be aware and consider necessary actions before continuing.
translation.HELP_RED_TEXT: Do not continue until the issue has been resolved.
translation.HELP_PAGE_GUIDELINES: You can use the guidelines at any time to read more about the eight steps.
translation.PRIVACY_PAGE_INTRO: The app is designed so that it can be used without you providing personal data.
translation.PRIVACY_PAGE_NO_DATA: At present, we do not collect, store, or share names, email addresses, phone numbers, or health information.
translation.PRIVACY_PAGE_LOCAL_STORAGE: Your answers are processed locally in the app while you use it. The app may save your progress in the decision tree locally on the device, in addition to a few technical choices such as selected language and whether introduction tips have been shown.
translation.PRIVACY_PAGE_EXTERNAL_LINKS: PDFs and external links may open in a browser or other apps. In those cases, the privacy terms of the external service may apply.
translation.PRIVACY_PAGE_UPDATES: If the app later collects results for statistics, you as a user will receive clear information on this page about what is collected, what it is used for, and that the information is anonymous. This privacy information will be updated before such a feature is used.
translation.CONTACT_PAGE_TEXT: The app was produced as part of a bachelor's project for the Information Systems programme at Østfold University College, and has since been further developed.
translation.DEFAULT_FEEDBACK_MESSAGE: Feedback missing - this is a test text.
